3.happening: Count the array slots describing the tree.
3.why: We need the size to allocate a status slot per node.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: The walk needs O(1) access to a node by its id.
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.happening: Start a status list: 0 = unseen, 1 = on the stack, 2 = visited.
6.why: It lets the tree colour each node by its progress.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its status.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a status.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order starting at slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned array slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.happening: Slot {i} holds a real value — build a node for it.
11.invariant: A null slot is skipped entirely.

11.why: null slots are placeholders for missing children.
11.invariant: No node is created for a null slot.

12.happening: Create node {i} with its value and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children (None).

13.happening: There's a slot ({child}) left to be a left child.
13.why: Children come from the next array slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

13.happening: No slot left for a left child.
13.why: The array is exhausted for this node.

14.happening: Slot {child} is a real node — it becomes the left child.
14.why: Only non-null slots become actual children.
14.invariant: left will point at a real child id.

14.happening: Slot {child} is null — no left child.
14.why: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as this node's left child.
15.why: It's the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.happening: Move past this slot whether or not it was a child.
16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: There's a slot ({child}) left to be a right child.
17.why: The right child is the slot right after the left one.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

17.happening: No slot left for a right child.
17.why: The array is exhausted for this node.

18.happening: Slot {child} is a real node — it becomes the right child.
18.why: Only non-null slots become actual children.
18.invariant: right will point at a real child id.

18.happening: Slot {child} is null — no right child.
18.why: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as this node's right child.
19.why: It's the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Record the node under its id for fast lookup.
22.why: The walk fetches nodes by id.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.why: It will collect every value in ascending (inorder) order.
23.invariant: order is the sorted sequence built so far.

24.happening: Create the empty stack that drives the inorder walk.
24.why: The stack remembers ancestors while we dive left.
24.invariant: The stack holds the path back up from cur.

25.why: Inorder begins by diving from the root.
25.invariant: cur is the node we're about to descend from; None means none.

26.happening: There's still a node to descend into or ancestors on the stack — keep going.
26.why: Inorder is done only when both cur is None and the stack is empty.
26.invariant: order holds every value visited so far, in ascending order.

26.happening: cur is None and the stack is empty — the whole tree is flattened.
26.invariant: order is the full sorted list of values.

27.happening: cur points at a node — push it and keep diving left.
27.why: The smallest unvisited value is the leftmost remaining node.
27.invariant: Everything left of cur is smaller and collected first.

27.happening: cur is None — the left spine ends here; time to pop.
27.why: There is no smaller node down this path.
27.invariant: The stack top is the next node in order.

28.why: We must return to it after finishing its left subtree.
28.invariant: The stack holds the path from the root to cur.

29.why: It is waiting to be visited after its left subtree.
29.invariant: Frontier nodes sit on the stack, not yet collected.

30.happening: Move cur to its left child.
30.why: We keep diving toward the smallest remaining value.
30.invariant: cur is None when there's no smaller node left.

31.happening: Pop the top of the stack — the next node in ascending order.
31.why: With no smaller node left, the top is the next-smallest.
31.invariant: cur is now the smallest uncollected node.

32.why: Its value is about to join the sorted sequence.
32.invariant: Visited nodes are never popped again.

33.happening: Read the value of the popped node.
33.why: We need it to append to the sorted order.
33.invariant: value is the current node's value.

34.why: order grows into the fully sorted sequence of values.
34.invariant: order holds the ascending values visited so far.

35.happening: Move cur to the popped node's right subtree.
35.why: Inorder continues with values larger than the node just collected.
35.invariant: The next-smallest uncollected node lies to the right (or up the stack).

36.why: order is fully sorted, so the (k-1)th index is the kth-smallest value.
36.invariant: answer now holds the final result.

37.why: The sorted order has been fully built and indexed.

init.happening: Building the BST from the array and preparing the stack and order list.
init.why: We need node objects, links, and status before the walk begins.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

loop.happening: Driving the inorder walk — dive left, then pop.
loop.why: Inorder visits BST values in ascending order.
loop.invariant: order holds every value visited so far, in ascending order.

update.happening: Pushing a node, marking status, or appending to order.
update.why: State must reflect the stack, visited nodes, and the growing sorted list.
update.invariant: Frontier nodes are on the stack; visited values are in order.

move.happening: Advancing cur — down-left, popping, or into the right subtree.
move.why: The cursor follows the inorder order of the BST.
move.invariant: cur is the next node to process (or None).

return.happening: Tree flattened — indexing order[k-1] and returning it.
